response.justification: Despite 15+ years of broad software engineering experience, this candidate's core expertise is in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang — not C++. The role demands deep, production-grade C++ and STL mastery as its primary requirement, yet the only C++ work on the resume is a 6-month stint at NI in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project. There is no evidence of multi-threaded systems, industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443), SDL practices, MQTT, or industrial-protocol experience, making this a poor fit for the role.
strengths: Demonstrated experience with high-performance, low-latency and distributed micro-services (Sociomantic: millions of HTTP req/s, async I/O, fibers)
Polyglot background with event-driven architecture and message-broker patterns evident in multiple roles
Consistent software craftsmanship — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, mentoring, and documentation across multiple employers
concerns: C++ exposure is superficial and dated: one 6-month role in 2014–2015 and a thesis project, far below the 8–12 years of C++ seniority required
Zero evidence of industrial cybersecurity knowledge, IEC 62443 standards, Secure Development Lifecycle, or security risk mitigation
No IoT, industrial automation, or industrial-protocol experience (MQTT, Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, IEC 61850, DNP 3.0)
